image_rule_inferred: 3x3 single-colour nonzero mask. The 9x9 output places the original 3x3 pattern in each 3x3 block whose corresponding input cell is nonzero; equivalently output[r*3+i][c*3+j] is colour iff input[r][c] and input[i][j] are both nonzero, else background inside 9x9 and all-zero outside 9x9.
target_requested: cost at least 10 lower than 102, ideally <=92 and preferably <=50
result: no candidate below cost 102 passed visible train/test/arc-gen plus fresh/margin requirements

main_attempts.idea: Remove Q vector to reach cost 92 while keeping current coordinate factors
main_attempts.why_promising: Q costs exactly 10 params; removing it would meet the requested -10 cost reduction.
main_attempts.finding: Rejected. Without Q/channel sign flip, the model becomes a zero-threshold same-channel linear separator. For k=2 masks, a false case containing source A plus an irrelevant cell conflicts with the true case containing A and B; no bias remains to separate them. Direct training of no-Q cost92 variants stayed far from visible/all-mask correctness.

main_attempts.idea: Use final threshold to linearize AND: sourceA + sourceB + bias instead of bilinear product
main_attempts.why_promising: The image rule only needs thresholded signs, so AND can be represented as x_A+x_B-1.5 if a channel-aware bias is available.
main_attempts.finding: The bias/channel flip is the hard part in a single zero-memory node. Q supplies that bias from background-vs-colour one-hot values; removing or compressing Q caused color/background conflicts, especially for color 9 and outside-row masking.

main_attempts.idea: Free learned H[2,9,3] coordinate embeddings with q0=-2
main_attempts.target_cost_if_direct: not adoptable because full 30-row H would cost 180 params, and 9x9 intermediate padding would cost much more than baseline
main_attempts.finding: A free H with rank 2 classified all 501 possible masks with positive margin, confirming the rule/linear-threshold idea. However, compressing that H into cost<=92 ONNX factors failed.

main_attempts.idea: Factor free H into low-cost one-Einsum coordinate decompositions
main_attempts.finding: All remained far from exact visible/all-mask correctness; best runs still had hundreds of mask-level failures, so they were not taken to official/fresh adoption.

A single small ConvTranspose can produce either block expansion or modulo-pattern expansion, not both. A kernel large enough to read both source positions is at least 11x11 and costs far above the baseline across channels. Two-branch Resize/Tile-style graphs require large 9x9/30x30 intermediates, making memory cost far above 102.

not_adopted_reason: No sub-102 candidate reached condition 1 (scripts/lib/scoring require_correct=True with visible train/test/arc-gen exact). Therefore no candidate was eligible for official decoder/fresh adoption.
honesty_note: The ONNX and full ZIP supplied with this report are the validated fallback, not an improved model.
